reviews.review_title: Please read this before anyone buys it
reviews.review_description: ￼ This game is fun at first until you realize it too late like me this game is no longer getting updated and is still stuck in the Christmas event from over a year ago. You don’t really notice it at first because you’re having fun slaying the zombies, but then you realize it when it’s too late to refund and you have realized the devs are no longer updating this so please do not buy this

The others were bras knuckles , a gun, and I forgot the other , but still they made it look like you had so many unique weapons to choose from and they didn’t even let you use them if! Same as my last review, this game isn’t bad may just be a skill issue but I don’t really recommend it also I’m pretty sure you could also get hacked in this one not to sure, but if you’re willing to take that risk go for it.

developer_response.body: Hi, sorry you didn't enjoy the game. There are a lot of weapons, you just have to unlock them. We focused on mastery a lot in the game's development so you could unlock and upgrade weapons to make it further and further. In regards to "getting hacked", that is just not the case.

reviews.review_title: Buggy but fun; !!DLC Guns do no dmg!!
reviews.review_description:
Don’t get me wrong the game may load like garbage but it is fun, the main problem is it severely lacks polish & optimization, & is bug ridden.

Be warned the dlc guns don’t work which needs to be fixed.

Arm tracking is kinda cheeks so the melee weapons aren’t really worth using, though some have nice effects.

The game is really best played with a buddy as the game sometimes doesn’t play fair & might bug out on ya leading to a dumb death.

The game can be played offline, there is no female character unfortunately for the ladies.

my gun keeps on glitching through the floor for no reason but the game is super fun but the friendly fire is annoying because tm8s keep killing you and dont revive you so please turn off but really fun and goofy game because the ragdolls

reviews.review_description: When i was ripunning from some of the zombies i tried to reload and it didn’t work. The reloading is very janky and barely works. I had to look at my hip to be able to reload.

it's pretty fun though

reviews.review_title: Maybe I just don't get it?
reviews.review_description: Thus game is terrible. Buggy, laggy, keep losing coop connection, quest 1 level aliasing and graphics, and most of all its just not fun. And it's low effort. It feels like a game someone submitted for their first homework assignment in "intro to game design". Maybe I just don't get it? This game for me feels like it would need polish before being free to play.
Really great game. Especially for only 9 dollars. Only problem I’ve had is when I’m holding a weapon and the physics goes wonky if it smacks into door frame when i’m walking.

Tldr: it's an atrocious rip off of the game "the cabin" 
Churned turd money grab of a game sadly.

Not to say this game isn't exactly fun but there are so many issues any fun you could have is almost sucked right out 
Firstly the visuals are atrocious I don't think I've seen something that bad even when I had a quest 2. Secondly the gameplay is low effort and clunky, good luck walking anywhere your weapon gets stuck on environment enemies and other players and warp out of existence. I got trapped in multiple spots on the map and the enemies never stop you give us so much stuff to do but no time whatsoever to do it. No menu for options in game. The holsters are near useless especially the front ones. And you need to add a holster adjuster my friend who is on the bigger side was having issues accessing them due to them being in his body.
This plays like a rough draft that maybe could sell reasonably for 5 dollars and that's a big maybe. You ripped off the game "the cabin" and managed to produce a disappointment and an eye sore. Only part y'all seemed to care about is the amount of weapons. Sucks I had hoped this would be a good game too, guess there's a reason the trailer is 90% blurred out

reviews.review_title: Not for me but could be for you
reviews.review_description: It made me think it was an open world Vr game instead it’s just cod zombies Vr but a little worse :/

reviews.review_title: A game that's fun but has some issues...
reviews.review_description:
The frame rate/performance and resolution definitely needs improvement. The weapons are good, but they could be better— no haptic feedback! I have no idea what’s up with that starter rifle; it has no impact and is completely useless. The holsters don’t work well either—8 out of 10 times when I try to store a weapon, I end up losing it. The maps, though, are great, with a lot of depth, and different areas can be unlocked over time. Unfortunately, the zombie AI is also not great, and the balance is off. Between waves, or even after a boss fight, the action continues immediately—no downtime at all.

And that’s my biggest gripe: you’re constantly rushed, running around, unable to stop! There’s no time to check things out or explore the map to see what areas are still locked, read the current objective, upgrade weapons or perks, or unlock crates—none of that—because a zombie is always right there. The most you can do is read or press something quickly while running by. It’s really frustrating. Just give us a little time after each wave, and a minute after a boss fight to prepare for the next phase. Those who want to start faster could just trigger it manually. I also miss having a pause button and a snap turn option.

I just hope some of these issues are taken seriously and worked on. At the moment, I can’t give it more than 3. UPDATE: No development here. Still the same issues. Sad. Down to 2 stars.

This might be the first game I give a 1-star review because it’s really that bad

(Important: The swarm of yellow zombies you see in the image doesn’t actually exist, and I only encountered a few of them. I’m very disappointed that they used such misleading images to lure players, and I fell for it. If you're looking for a similar experience, I recommend playing "After the Fall" instead.)

(Short review: Play another game instead. For this price, you can find other games that are much more fun.)

Long review:

First, let me mention the positives of Frenzy Extinction. The maps are beautiful, there’s a variety of weapons, and the game includes boss fights. But those are the only positives. Now, onto the negatives:

1. Terrible game sounds: It greatly detracts from the gameplay experience. For example, the sound of a pistol is louder than that of a rifle, the gun sounds lack the heavy charm of a real weapon, and the boss has very little sound, among other issues.

2. Boring boss fights: Just picture this: "You run around the boss -> you shoot the boss -> repeat." That's all there is. The bosses don’t feel like actual bosses at all.

3. Gameplay lacks excitement: In a zombie game, it’s crucial to feel the thrill of survival or the adrenaline of shooting hordes of zombies coming at you, but this game lacks both.

4. Most vending machines in the game are unusable: I understand there’s a progression system that unlocks things later in the game and character upgrades in levels, but imagine playing for the first time and using the same gun for almost half an hour to finish a level. Your first impression will already be ruined. You have thousands of in-game currency, but you can’t buy anything.


I don’t think I need to write more about this because this game is genuinely bad, especially for the price.

Don’t bump into another player or drop your weapon. If you do it’s gone and good luck finishing the level without it. 

Game had potential but the glitching makes it a beta not a finished game that you pay for.

Frankly I think I could have built it in horizon worlds.
It's so silly, I punched a boss out of existence. 10/10. Also I don't think anyone will see this so I admit to loving garlic bread

I’m not just saying this so I can win frenzy extinction. This game is fun, and even once you beat it you can play it more and more and unlock and customize you wide variety of weapons. Not just that, I also love the cosmetics, they are so cool and the outfits are even better. The DLC’s will even be free! This game is very much worth the 20$ price tag.

It is even more dissapointing to see what complete crao meta let through these days and that they dont even follow the policy of allowing refunds anymore. That for sure will keep me from buying any new experiences on meta!

Such a big let down and dissapointment meta has become.

This game looks interesting and it seem promising when reading the reviews and game descriptions, but it must be bought reviews because its a complete letdown and crap game.

STAY AWAY!

*Frenzy Extinction* delivers an intense, fast-paced VR survival experience. From the moment you step into its post-apocalyptic world, you're thrown into a relentless battle against hordes of infected enemies, known as "The Ridden." The game excels in chaotic, horde-based gameplay that keeps you constantly on the move, upgrading your weapons and adapting to the increasingly difficult waves of enemies.

One of the standout features is the variety of content: over 35 weapons to customize, 30 unique enemies to face, and more than 10 maps that each present new challenges. The weapon and enemy variety, combined with randomized upgrades and waves, ensure no two runs feel the same. The action is fast and brutal, with full-body dismemberment mechanics adding a visceral edge to the combat.

What really elevates *Frenzy Extinction* is the multiplayer mode. Teaming up with up to three friends, you can coordinate your strategies to survive the waves, adding a layer of tactical depth. The co-op aspect is key, as solo play can feel overwhelmingly difficult at times, but with friends, the game turns into a chaotic, adrenaline-pumping ride.

Graphically, the game does well in terms of VR immersion, with detailed environments and smooth animations, though some maps feel more polished than others. The sound design complements the action, with satisfying audio feedback for each weapon and eerie ambient noises that keep you on edge.

While the game provides endless replayability with its random upgrade system and challenging enemy waves, the difficulty can sometimes feel too steep for casual players. It's clear that *Frenzy Extinction* is aimed at VR gamers who crave high-energy, intense survival gameplay. Amazing game great work-Reyreyvr
